description: Shared Sense360 firmware / entity framework contract: the stable capability registry, the module-status and device-health value contracts, and the per-config-string compile-time capability declarations that products/bundles/*.yaml implement via s360_* substitutions and packages/base/device_framework.yaml. Docs describe; this file decides. Validated by tests/test_core_framework.py.
guardrails: All capability and module-status values in this contract are compile-time composition facts only: no runtime hardware autodetection is performed or claimed by the framework.
Module status 'Included' is compile-time presence, never a health, availability, bench or hardware claim.
The reserved runtime status values (Initialising / Available / Degraded / Unavailable / Fault) may only be introduced by a module-specific PR that wires a real supported runtime signal.
This contract changes no release declaration: config/webflash-builds.json rows, channels, versions, artifact names, product lifecycle and commercial state are untouched.
Compile-only skeletons (products/compile-only/) and the provisioning bench harness (tests/bench/) never include the framework package, so they cannot be mistaken for customer products.
Firmware-build proof only: nothing in this contract claims hardware, bench, compliance, safety or commercial-availability proof.

Ceiling-POE-FanPWM is the one declared framework gap (framework_included: false): its bundle is pinned package-identical to the native full-compile-validated skeleton, so the framework include is deferred until that lane re-records. All other configs compose the framework exactly once.

core.description: Sense360 Core hub board (ESP32-S3). Present in every composition; owns WiFi/API/OTA base infrastructure.

power_poe.description: PoE power composition: the S360-410 PoE PSU package is compiled in (power-source, supply-voltage and PoE-status entities).

power_usb.description: USB power composition: the USB power package (packages/hardware/power_usb.yaml) is compiled in (power-source and supply-voltage entities); no PSU module package is composed.

roomiq.description: RoomIQ comfort-sensing firmware compiled from the RoomIQ climate half (SHT4x temperature/humidity + VEML7700 ambient light) on the S360-200 board. Describes compiled firmware only, not the board BOM.

airiq.description: AirIQ air-quality firmware (SCD4x CO2, SGP4x VOC/NOx, SPS30 particulate, BMP3xx pressure) on the S360-210 board. The MICS-4514 gas stage exists on the hardware but is not compiled in any current configuration and is NOT part of this capability.

ventiq.description: VentIQ bathroom air-quality firmware (SGP4x VOC/NOx, SHT4x temperature/humidity, BMP3xx pressure) on the S360-211 board. Describes compiled firmware only.

presence.description: Presence firmware compiled from the RoomIQ radar half: HLK-LD2450 mmWave occupancy/target tracking on the S360-200 board's radar UART. PIR and SEN0609 inputs exist on the hardware but are not compiled in any current configuration and are NOT part of this capability.

led.description: S360-300 LED ring firmware: WS2812 RMT LED-strip driver and effects.

fan_relay.description: S360-310 on/off relay fan driver (experimental lane).

fan_pwm.description: S360-311 12V PWM fan driver with tach feedback.

fan_triac.description: S360-320 TRIAC phase dimmer (experimental self-build mains lane; never stable / buyable).

values.Not included: The module's packages are not part of this configuration's compiled composition. This is the normal state for an optional module that was not ordered — it is never a fault.
values.Included: Compile-time presence only: the module's packages are compiled into this firmware. It does not mean the module was detected, is healthy, or is available — runtime health signals arrive with the module-specific PRs.

reserved_values.Initialising: Reserved: module is present and inside its startup warm-up window; must not be reported as Unavailable during warm-up.
reserved_values.Available: Reserved: module is present and a real supported runtime signal (successful sensor update, component status, or communication success) confirms it is working. Never derived from compilation alone.
reserved_values.Degraded: Reserved: module responds but part of its function is impaired (e.g. one of several sensors failing or stale). Must not mark unrelated modules unavailable.
reserved_values.Unavailable: Reserved: module is expected (compiled in) but a real runtime signal shows it is not responding after warm-up.
reserved_values.Fault: Reserved: module reports an explicit error condition. Never used for a module that is simply absent.

values.Starting: Device is inside the boot warm-up window (s360_health_warmup_seconds, default 120 s). Startup must not be reported as a fault.
values.Running: Base firmware completed its startup window and no framework-level fault signal exists. This states nothing about modules or sensors: no module runtime-health signal is connected yet, and no physical sensor has been verified.

reserved_values.Healthy: Reserved: every included module reports real runtime health (module-specific PRs). Never derived from the warm-up window or from compilation alone.
reserved_values.Degraded: Reserved: at least one included module is Degraded or Unavailable while the rest of the device works.
reserved_values.Fault: Reserved: a critical module or the device itself reports an explicit fault.
reserved_values.Safe mode: Reserved: the device booted into ESPHome safe mode / recovery. Requires a supported runtime signal before it can be reported.

reserved_inputs: stale-sensor-data detection (distinguished from a missing module)

Ceiling-POE-FanPWM.deferral_reason: Deferred: products/bundles/ceiling-poe-fanpwm.yaml must compose the IDENTICAL package set as the native full-compile-validated compile-only skeleton products/compile-only/ceiling-poe-fanpwm-native.yaml (S360-311-NATIVE-FANPWM-COMPILE-001, tests/test_pwm_product_readiness.py). Adding the framework package would break that identity and silently invalidate the recorded native full-compile evidence. The framework include lands for this config in a follow-up once the native compile-only lane re-records a framework-bearing composition.
